root_text.an4.140:1.3: Atthi, bhikkhave, vādī atthato pariyādānaṁ gacchati, no byañjanato;
root_text.an4.140:1.4: atthi, bhikkhave, vādī byañjanato pariyādānaṁ gacchati, no atthato;
root_text.an4.140:1.5: atthi, bhikkhave, vādī atthato ca byañjanato ca pariyādānaṁ gacchati;
root_text.an4.140:1.6: atthi, bhikkhave, vādī nevatthato no byañjanato pariyādānaṁ gacchati.
root_text.an4.140:1.8: Aṭṭhānametaṁ, bhikkhave, anavakāso yaṁ catūhi paṭisambhidāhi samannāgato atthato vā byañjanato vā pariyādānaṁ gaccheyyā”ti.

translation_text.an4.140:1.3: There’s a speaker who runs out of meaningful things to say, but not of ways of phrasing things.
translation_text.an4.140:1.4: There’s a speaker who runs out of ways of phrasing things, but not of meaningful things to say.
translation_text.an4.140:1.5: There’s a speaker who runs out of both meaningful things to say, and ways of phrasing things.
translation_text.an4.140:1.6: There’s a speaker who never runs out of meaningful things to say, or ways of phrasing things.
translation_text.an4.140:1.8: It is impossible, it cannot happen that someone accomplished in the four kinds of textual analysis will ever run out of meaningful things to say, or ways of phrasing things.”
